parts.title: The Summer Storm
parts.text: Every summer, it was a family **tradition** for Priya and her cousins to visit their grandparents' farm. Priya's grandmother was always **watchful** — her sharp eyes noticed every detail, from a missing chicken to a crack in the fence. One morning, a **fearsome** storm blew through the valley, so terrifying that the horses kicked at their stalls.

parts.title: The Damage
parts.text: When the sky cleared, the **wreckage** of the old shed lay scattered across the yard — broken boards, bent nails, and shattered glass everywhere. Priya let out a **gasp** when she saw the damage. Her grandmother stayed calm. "Standing still won't help," she said. "We need to be **active** and start cleaning up right away." Mateo was **defiant** at first, crossing his arms and saying it was too much work.

parts.title: The Rebuild
parts.text: But Priya had the **ambition** to rebuild the shed better than before. She knew that being the oldest gave her an **advantage** — the younger kids always followed her lead. She found leftover wood at a neighbor's house for a great **bargain** — almost free. She used cold water to **revive** the wilted flowers that the storm had knocked over. Her hard work seemed to **imply** that she would never give up, no matter what.

parts.title: The Reward
parts.text: By the end of the week, the new shed stood taller and stronger than the old one. Grandma hugged Priya and said, "You showed everyone that a mess is just a beginning, not an ending." That night, the whole family sat on the porch eating watermelon, and Priya decided that next summer she would build a treehouse too.

definitions.meaning1: To bring back to life, health, or strength. Example: The gardener used water and sunlight to revive the dying plant.
definitions.meaning2: To bring back into use or popularity. Example: The school decided to revive the old tradition of a spring dance.
definitions.studentChallenge: After a long nap, a cold glass of lemonade was enough to ___ her energy.

definitions.meaning1: A custom or belief passed down through generations. Example: Eating turkey on Thanksgiving is a tradition in many families.
definitions.studentChallenge: Passing down grandma's recipe is a ___ that has lasted for three generations.

definitions.meaning1: Paying close attention to what is happening; alert. Example: The watchful mother eagle kept her eyes on the nest.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ guard stood at the entrance, checking every bag.

definitions.meaning1: The broken pieces of something that has been destroyed. Example: After the tornado, the wreckage of the barn was scattered across the field.
definitions.studentChallenge: Rescue workers searched through the ___ for survivors.

definitions.meaning1: Something that helps you or puts you in a better position. Example: Being tall gives a basketball player a big advantage.
definitions.studentChallenge: She took ___ of the quiet library to finish her homework early.

definitions.meaning1: A strong desire to achieve something. Example: Her ambition was to become the first person in her family to go to college.
definitions.studentChallenge: The young inventor had the ___ to build a robot before she turned twelve.

definitions.meaning1: Boldly refusing to obey or follow rules. Example: The defiant child crossed her arms and refused to apologize.
definitions.studentChallenge: Even when the principal called her name, she remained ___ and would not sit down.

definitions.meaning1: Very frightening or scary. Example: The dragon in the movie was the most fearsome creature the children had ever seen.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ storm uprooted trees and sent trash cans flying.

definitions.meaning1: To suggest something without saying it directly. Example: Her tone seemed to imply that she did not agree.
definitions.studentChallenge: His smile did not ___ that anything was wrong, but his eyes told a different story.

definitions.meaning1: Moving around a lot; busy and energetic. Example: The puppy was so active that it never stopped running and jumping.
definitions.meaning2: Currently working or in operation. Example: The volcano is still active — scientists say it could erupt at any time.
definitions.studentChallenge: My grandmother stays ___ by walking two miles every morning.

definitions.meaning1: Something bought for less than the usual price; a good deal. Example: The lamp for only two dollars was a great bargain.
definitions.meaning2: To negotiate the price of something. Example: The customer tried to bargain over the price of the old chair.
definitions.studentChallenge: At the yard sale, we found a beautiful bookshelf for five dollars — what a ___!

definitions.meaning1: To take a quick, sharp breath, usually from surprise or shock. Example: The audience let out a gasp when the magician pulled a rabbit from the empty hat.
definitions.studentChallenge: The diver came up from underwater and took a huge ___ of fresh air.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The gardener used water and sunlight to _______ the dying plant back to health.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: After a long nap, a cold glass of lemonade was enough to _______ her energy.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Eating turkey on Thanksgiving is a _______ in many American families.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Passing down grandma's recipe is a _______ that has lasted for three generations.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ mother eagle kept her eyes on the nest from the highest branch.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: A _______ guard stood at the museum entrance, checking every bag that passed through.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: After the tornado passed, the _______ of the barn was scattered across the entire field.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Rescue workers searched through the _______ for survivors.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Being tall gives a basketball player a big _______ over shorter opponents.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She took _______ of the quiet library to finish her homework early.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Her _______ was to become the first person in her family to go to college.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The young inventor had the _______ to build a robot before she turned twelve.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ child crossed her arms and refused to apologize.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Even when the principal called her name, she remained _______ and would not sit down.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The dragon in the movie was the most _______ creature the children had ever seen.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The _______ storm uprooted trees and sent trash cans flying down the street.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: When she said "That's an interesting choice," her tone seemed to _______ that she did not agree.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: His smile did not _______ that anything was wrong, but his eyes told a different story.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The puppy was so _______ that it never stopped running, jumping, and chasing its tail.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The volcano is still _______ — scientists say it could erupt at any time.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: At the flea market, my mom found a beautiful lamp for only two dollars — what a _______!
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The store owner and the customer tried to _______ over the price of the old chair.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: When the magician pulled a rabbit from the empty hat, the audience let out a _______.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The diver came up from underwater and took a huge _______ of fresh air.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Every summer, it was a family _(1)_ for Priya and her cousins to visit their grandparents' farm. Priya's grandmother was always _(2)_ — her sharp eyes noticed every detail, from a missing chicken to a crack in the fence.

One morning, a _(3)_ storm blew through the valley, so terrifying that the horses kicked at their stalls. When the sky cleared, the _(4)_ of the old shed lay scattered across the yard — broken boards, bent nails, and shattered glass everywhere.

Priya let out a _(5)_ when she saw the damage — she could barely catch her breath. Her grandmother stayed calm. "Standing still won't help," she said. "We need to be _(6)_ and start cleaning up right away."

Priya's cousin was _(7)_ at first, crossing his arms and saying it was too much work. But Priya had the _(8)_ to rebuild the shed better than before. She knew that being the oldest gave her an _(9)_ — the younger kids always followed her lead.

She found leftover wood at a neighbor's house for a great _(10)_ — almost free. She used cold water to _(11)_ the wilted flowers that the storm had knocked over. Her hard work seemed to _(12)_ that she would never give up, no matter what.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
